steps.title: Use the study result
steps.content: The passage says limited logging plots may be more robust than plots that have not been logged. That finding challenges the idea that logging is always opposed to forest conservation.

steps.title: State the careful conclusion
steps.content: B compares limited logging with other strategies the study does not discuss. C says benefits will be hard to know, but the study already suggests one. D contradicts the finding by favoring untouched forests. The careful inference is that limited logging may help forest health. The answer is A.
